title: Women's Support Groups - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Mental & Behavioral Health > Women's Support Groups 3 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF CONSEJO HISPANO MUJERES APOYANDO MUJERES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF A support group providing a safe space to talk with other women. Topics include healthy relationships and domestic violence. Zumba is offered with a certified instructor. Snacks, refreshments, and childcare are provided during the group. 565 12th Street Astoria, OR 97103 Eligibility: Individuals who identify as female and their children Hours: Thursday 5:30pm-7:30pm Bob Chisholm Community Center 1225 Avenue A Seaside, OR 97138 Email (503) 325-4547 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS OF OREGON NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS CLACKAMAS NAMI SUPPORT GROUPS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health support groups. The groups are facilitated by trained volunteers with a focus on LGBTQ2+ individuals, veterans, families, and parents. 10202 SE 32nd Avenue Suite 501 Milwaukie, OR 97222 Eligibility: Individuals with mental health conditions and their families, support groups may have additional requirements Hours: Varies by support group Email (503) 344-5050 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PATHFINDER NETWORK PATHFINDER NETWORK CENTER FOR FAMILY SUCCESS HEALING TRAUMA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Skill-based support group for women who have experienced adversity, stress and trauma. Meals are provided and childcare is available. 7305 NE Glisan Street Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Female-identified persons that have experienced trauma Hours: Thursday 3pm-5pm (these are rotating 6 week sessions, please call for current class schedule) Email (503) 892-5396 1008 Get Directions Visit Website More Details CONSEJO HISPANO MUJERES APOYANDO MUJERES NATIONAL ALLIANCE ON MENTAL ILLNESS OF OREGON NAMI SUPPORT GROUPS PATHFINDER NETWORK HEALING TRAUMA
